# fptcloud_floating_ip_association (Resource)

Provides a FPT cloud floating ip which can be associate and disassociate floating ip from instances.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fptcloud_floating_ip" "example" {
  vpc_id = "your_vpc_id"
}

resource "fptcloud_floating_ip_association" "association_example" {
  vpc_id         = "your_vpc_id"
  floating_ip_id = fptcloud_floating_ip.example.id
  instance_id    = "your_instance_id"
  depends_on = [fptcloud_floating_ip.example]
}
```

## Import

Import format: `vpc_id/floating_ip_id`. Use `fptcloud_vpc` for `vpc_id` and `fptcloud_floating_ip` (with filter by id or ip_address) for the floating IP `id`, then use a separate resource and an `import` block.

**Example: import using data sources**

```terraform
data "fptcloud_vpc" "main" {
  name = "my-vpc"
}

data "fptcloud_floating_ip" "imported" {
  vpc_id = data.fptcloud_vpc.main.id
  filter {
    key    = "ip_address"
    values = ["floating-ip"]
  }
}

import {
  to = fptcloud_floating_ip_association.imported_fip
  id = "${data.fptcloud_vpc.main.id}/${element(data.fptcloud_floating_ip.imported.floating_ips, 0).id}"
}

resource "fptcloud_floating_ip_association" "imported_fip" {
  vpc_id         = data.fptcloud_vpc.main.id
  floating_ip_id = element(data.fptcloud_floating_ip.imported.floating_ips, 0).id
  instance_id = element(data.fptcloud_floating_ip.imported.floating_ips, 0).instance_id
}
```

After import, run `terraform plan`; state will match the API. Remove the `import` block once done. CLI: `terraform import fptcloud_floating_ip_association.imported "<vpc_id>/<floating_ip_id>"`.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `floating_ip_id` (String) The id of the ip address
- `vpc_id` (String) The vpc id of the floating ip

### Optional

- `floating_ip_port` (Number) The port of the floating ip
- `instance_id` (String) The id of the instance
- `instance_port` (Number) The port of the instance

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
